propositions: _acquire_fcntl catches every OSError from fcntl.flock identically, masking genuine system faults as a spurious LockTimeout.
Contention errnos EAGAIN/EACCES should retry until the deadline then close the fd and return the -1 timeout sentinel, unchanged.
Non-contention errnos like ENOLCK/EIO/EBADF should close the fd and re-raise the OSError immediately, preserving its errno.
No EINTR branch is needed because PEP 475 retries interrupted syscalls at the C level so OSError(EINTR) never surfaces.
The file descriptor must be closed on every non-success exit path, including both the timeout return and the re-raise.
Add direct tests of _acquire_fcntl injecting specific errnos by monkeypatching the module's fcntl.flock and spying on os.close.
